steps.title: Match the plural noun
steps.content: The blank modifies the plural noun "nurses" and refers back to the thousands of nurses mentioned in the previous sentence. The modifier must therefore be plural.

steps.title: Use plural demonstrative agreement
steps.content: Choice A would create "one nurses," which is ungrammatical. Choice B and Choice C are singular demonstratives, so they cannot modify the plural noun "nurses." The plural demonstrative correctly agrees with the noun and points back to the group already named. The answer is D.
